description: Mixed Python package management — three tools coexist with conflicting versions and ranges
review_checklist: Audit binary vs source variants of equivalent packages

ground_truth.description: Mixed package managers: requirements.txt (pip), Pipfile (pipenv), and pyproject.toml (Poetry) all coexist with DIFFERENT version constraints for the same packages. Flask is 2.3.3 / 3.0.0 / ^3.1 across the three. Whichever tool runs last 'wins' — this is a guaranteed dev/prod divergence. Choose one tool, delete the others.

psycopg2 (source build) requires PostgreSQL development headers and a C compiler — fails in production containers without those installed. The Pipfile correctly uses psycopg2-binary; align both files to the binary variant for production.
Pipfile gunicorn = "*" allows any version. In production, this means installs are not reproducible; a new release can break the deploy. Pin to an exact version or version range.
Caret range ^3.1 for Flask allows 3.1.0 through <4.0.0. Combined with the other manifests' tighter pins, the installed version depends on which tool the deployer ran. Drift inevitable.
